meta_title: For Students: user onboarding to Solve can't finish projects (Beginner Case Study)
meta_description: Learn how to user onboarding and overcome can't finish projects. A Beginner-level Case Study using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Students: User Onboarding to Solve Project Completion
content.seo_description: Stop abandoning projects. Learn how to implement user onboarding to finish your first app using Antigravity and Claude Code in this expert case study.
content.answer_block: The root cause of unfinished projects is a lack of user-focused momentum. By implementing a frictionless onboarding flow first, you create a tangible 'win' that anchors your development. This modular approach keeps you focused, provides immediate user value, and transforms a daunting codebase into a series of achievable, rewarding milestones.
content.intro:
Your desktop is a graveyard of half-baked repositories. You start with ambition, but the complexity of authentication, database schemas, and state management eventually suffocates your momentum. The feeling of 'stuck' isn't a lack of talent—it's a lack of structure.

Professional developers don't build apps; they build user experiences. By shifting your focus to user onboarding, you solve the most critical part of your app first. This isn't just about UI; it's about defining the value proposition. Once a user can sign up and reach their first 'Aha!' moment, the rest of the project shifts from a chore to a guided mission.

steps.title: Define the 'Aha!' Moment
steps.description: Before writing code, define the single action that delivers value. This prevents scope creep and keeps you focused on the user's journey.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze the core value proposition of a task-management app and identify the single most important action a user should take during onboarding to experience immediate success.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Create a brief design document for the onboarding flow focusing on a single, high-impact user action.'

steps.title: Bootstrap the Auth Layer
steps.description: Standardize your authentication so you don't spend weeks building login forms. Use pre-built components to move fast.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a secure, minimalist authentication flow using NextAuth.js that redirects users immediately to the onboarding wizard upon successful login.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Implement NextAuth.js and create a protected route for the onboarding wizard.'

steps.title: Build the Onboarding Wizard
steps.description: Create a multi-step form that gathers essential user data without overwhelming them. Keep it under three steps.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a 3-step onboarding wizard component with progress tracking, validation, and optimistic UI updates for a React-based application.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Generate a React wizard component with three steps: profile setup, preference selection, and tutorial tour.'

steps.title: Persist User Progress
steps.description: Ensure that if a user leaves, they can return exactly where they left off. This reduces drop-off rates significantly.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Design a schema for user onboarding status in Prisma that tracks completion percentage and current step index.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Update the database schema to store onboarding state and create an API endpoint to save progress.'

steps.title: Trigger the 'First Value' Event
steps.description: Automate the transition from onboarding to the main dashboard. This creates a psychological closure point for the user.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a hook that detects when a user completes the onboarding wizard and triggers a welcome email and a redirect to the main app dashboard.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Implement a completion handler for the wizard that redirects the user to the dashboard and triggers a welcome notification.'

pro_tips: Never build an app without a 'Minimum Viable Onboarding' path; it forces you to prioritize features.
Use optimistic UI updates for your onboarding forms to make the app feel instantaneous, even on slow connections.
Limit your input fields to three or fewer; every extra field is a 20% drop-off risk for new users.
Treat your onboarding code as a separate module; it makes testing and iteration significantly easier.
Always include a 'skip' option; power users will appreciate the freedom to explore your app on their own terms.
content.next_steps: After completing your onboarding flow, deploy your app to Vercel and share your link in the community channel to get feedback from other builders.
content.conversion_block: Stop letting your ideas die in a private repository. Join a community that prioritizes shipping, feedback, and real-world results. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
